name: com.coachfit.sync.adapter.in.GarminJobConsumer
comment:
Redis Stream consumer for Garmin push processing jobs.

Reads from the shared {@code sync:jobs} stream, consumer group {@code sync-workers}, under consumer name {@code garmin-worker-1}. Strava jobs (handled by {@link StravaJobConsumer} as {@code worker-1}) use a different consumer name so both consumers compete for undelivered messages without stepping on each other.

Dispatch table:

  • Health types → {@link GarminHealthProcessingService}
  • Activity types → {@link GarminActivitySyncService}
  • Non-garmin provider → skip (leave for Strava consumer)

Error handling:

  • Transient failures: ack + re-enqueue with incremented attempt
  • After {@value MAX_ATTEMPTS}: move to {@code sync:dead_letter}
  • Pending recovery: every 5 minutes via {@link #recoverPendingMessages()}

See docs/06-sync-engine-spec.md §Redis Stream Design.

methods.name: initConsumerGroup
methods.comment: Ensures the consumer group exists. The group is also created by {@link StravaJobConsumer}; this is idempotent — duplicate BUSYGROUP errors are silently swallowed.

methods.name: pollJobs
methods.comment:
Polls the {@code sync:jobs} stream every second for new Garmin messages.

Only processes messages where {@code provider = 'garmin'}. Others are left unacked for the Strava consumer. Note: because Strava consumer acks non-strava and Garmin consumer acks non-garmin, both independently skip messages for the other provider.

methods.name: recoverPendingMessages
methods.comment: Every 5 minutes: reclaims messages pending in the Garmin consumer's backlog for more than 60 seconds (crash-recovery).

name: com.coachfit.sync.application.service.GarminHealthProcessingService
comment:
Worker service: processes Garmin health push jobs from the Redis Stream.

Handled job types:

  • {@code garmin_dailies} → upsert {@code health_daily_summaries}, autofill wellness
  • {@code garmin_sleep} → upsert {@code health_sleep_data}, autofill wellness
  • {@code garmin_body} → upsert weight into {@code health_daily_summaries} + wellness
  • {@code garmin_stress} → aggregate daily avg stress → {@code health_daily_summaries}
  • {@code garmin_hrv} → upsert HRV → {@code health_sleep_data}, autofill wellness
  • {@code garmin_pulseox} → aggregate avg SpO2 → {@code health_daily_summaries}
  • {@code garmin_respiration} → aggregate avg respiration → {@code health_daily_summaries}
  • {@code garmin_user_metrics}→ VO2max → {@code health_daily_summaries}

Activities and activity-details are handled by {@link GarminActivitySyncService}.

Each {@code process*} method is transactional — the persistence write and wellness autofill happen atomically within one transaction.

See docs/06-sync-engine-spec.md §Health Data Processing.

methods.name: processDailies
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_dailies} job.

Garmin payload fields (partial — all fields optional): 

 { "calendarDate":           "2025-03-15", "startTimeInSeconds":     1710460800, "steps":                  12500, "distanceInMeters":       9234.5, "activeKilocalories":     800, "bmrKilocalories":        1800, "restingHeartRateInBeatsPerMinute": 52, "averageStressLevel":     28, "maxStressLevel":         65, "bodyBatteryChargedValue": 45, "bodyBatteryDrainedValue": 32, "highlyActiveSeconds":    1800, "activeSeconds":          3600, "floorsClimbed":          12, "averageSpO2":            96.5, "averageRespirationValue": 14.2 }

Autofill path: resting_hr and stress_level are written to {@code wellness_logs} for the same date (docs/06-sync-engine-spec.md §Dailies push).

methods.name: processSleep
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_sleep} job.

Garmin payload fields (partial): 

 { "calendarDate":            "2025-03-15", "startTimeInSeconds":      1710432000, "durationInSeconds":       27000, "deepSleepDurationInSeconds": 5400, "lightSleepDurationInSeconds": 16200, "remSleepInSeconds":       3600, "awakeDurationInSeconds":  1800, "sleepScores":             {"overall": {"value": 82}}, "averageRespirationValue": 14.1, "averageSpO2Value":        97.0, "averageHRV":              45.2, "hrvStatus":               "BALANCED" }

Autofill path: sleep_hours, sleep_quality, hrv written to {@code wellness_logs} (docs/06-sync-engine-spec.md §Sleep push).

methods.name: processBodyComposition
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_body} job.

Garmin payload fields (partial): 

 { "measurementTimeInSeconds": 1710460800, "weightInGrams":           75000, "bodyFatPercentage":       18.5, "bodyMassIndex":           23.1 }

Weight is written to {@code health_daily_summaries.extra} and {@code wellness_logs.weight_kg}.

methods.name: processStress
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_stress} job.

Garmin stress payload contains per-sample stress readings throughout the day: 

 { "startTimeInSeconds": 1710460800, "startTimeOffsetInSeconds": 25200, "stressLevel": 35, "bodyBattery": { "charged": 20, "drained": 15 } }

A stress detail push can be a single sample or represent a stress summary. We upsert the {@code avg_stress} and optionally {@code body_battery} into {@code health_daily_summaries} for the date derived from {@code startTimeInSeconds}.

methods.name: processHrv
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_hrv} job.

Garmin HRV payload shape: 

 { "startTimeInSeconds": 1710432000, "hrvSummary": { "weeklyAvg":      45, "lastNight":      42, "lastNight5MinHigh": 55, "status":         "BALANCED" } }

Upserts {@code health_sleep_data.avg_hrv} and auto-updates {@code wellness_logs.hrv} for the night date.

methods.name: processPulseOx
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_pulseox} job.

Garmin Pulse Ox payload shape: 

 { "startTimeInSeconds": 1710460800, "spo2Value":          96.5, "onDemand":           false }

Upserts average SpO2 into {@code health_daily_summaries.avg_spo2}.

methods.name: processRespiration
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_respiration} job.

Garmin respiration payload shape (epoch summary): 

 { "startTimeInSeconds":       1710460800, "respirationEpochSummary": [ { "startTimeInSeconds": ..., "respirationValue": 13.5 }, ... ] }

Computes average respiration and upserts into {@code health_daily_summaries.avg_respiration}.

methods.name: processUserMetrics
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_user_metrics} job.

Garmin user metrics payload shape: 

 { "calendarDate":  "2025-03-15", "vo2Max":        48.0, "fitnessAge":    32, "trainingStatus": "MAINTAINING" }

Upserts VO2max into {@code health_daily_summaries.vo2max}. Training status and fitness age go into {@code extra}.

methods.name: extractDate
methods.comment: Extracts the date from a Garmin payload. Prefers {@code calendarDate} (ISO date string); falls back to {@code startTimeInSeconds} epoch → UTC date.

methods.name: extractSleepScore
methods.comment: Extracts the sleep score integer from the nested {@code sleepScores.overall.value} path. Returns null if the structure is absent.

methods.name: computeAvgRespiration
methods.comment: Computes average respiration from an epoch summary array. Returns null if no readings are present.

methods.name: buildExtra
methods.comment: Builds a JSON string of extra provider-specific fields — all fields in the data map that are NOT in the excluded standard-column set.

methods.name: buildBodyExtra
methods.comment: Builds body composition extra JSON.

methods.name: normalizeStress1to5
methods.comment:
Normalises Garmin average stress (0–100) to wellness scale (1–5).
  • 0–25 → 1 (low)
  • 26–50 → 2
  • 51–65 → 3
  • 66–80 → 4
  • 81–100 → 5 (high)

methods.name: normalizeSleepScore1to5
methods.comment:
Normalises Garmin sleep score (0–100) to wellness scale (1–5).
  • ≥80 → 5 (excellent)
  • 65–79 → 4 (good)
  • 50–64 → 3 (fair)
  • 35–49 → 2 (poor)
  • <35 → 1 (very poor)

methods.name: normalizeHrvStatus
methods.comment: Normalises Garmin HRV status string to schema values. Schema: balanced / low / unbalanced (docs/04-db-schema.md §health_sleep_data).

methods.name: buildFieldSources
methods.comment:
Builds the {@code field_sources} JSON for wellness_logs merge. Only non-null field names are included.

Example output: {@code {"resting_hr":"garmin","stress_level":"garmin"}}

methods.name: toMinutes
methods.comment: Converts a nullable seconds value to whole minutes (floor). Returns null if input is null.

methods.name: processEpochs
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_epochs} job.

Garmin epoch payload fields:

  • {@code startTimeInSeconds} — epoch start (UTC unix seconds)
  • {@code durationInSeconds} — typically 900
  • {@code steps} — step count in this epoch
  • {@code activeKilocalories} — kcal burned
  • {@code met} — metabolic equivalent
  • {@code intensity} — SEDENTARY / ACTIVE / HIGHLY_ACTIVE / etc.
  • {@code movingDurationInSeconds} — seconds actively moving
  • {@code distance} — meters

methods.name: processBloodPressure
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_blood_pressure} job.

Stores readings in {@code health_daily_summaries.extra} under key {@code blood_pressure_readings}. Only devices with BP sensors push this type.

Garmin payload fields:

  • {@code startTimeInSeconds} — reading timestamp
  • {@code systolic} — systolic mmHg
  • {@code diastolic} — diastolic mmHg
  • {@code pulse} — pulse bpm at time of reading

methods.name: processMenstrualCycle
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_menstrual_cycle} job.

Stores cycle phase data in {@code health_daily_summaries.extra} under key {@code menstrual_cycle}. Used for cycle-aware training plan adjustments.

Garmin payload fields:

  • {@code calendarDate} — date string (YYYY-MM-DD)
  • {@code cycleDay} — day within current cycle
  • {@code phase} — current phase (MENSTRUAL/FOLLICULAR/OVULATORY/LUTEAL)
  • {@code predictedPhase} — predicted phase if actual unknown

methods.name: processPregnancy
methods.comment:
Processes a {@code garmin_pregnancy} job.

Stores pregnancy data in {@code health_daily_summaries.extra} under key {@code pregnancy}.

Garmin payload fields:

  • {@code weeksPregnant} — weeks of gestation
  • {@code dueDate} — expected due date (YYYY-MM-DD)

name: com.coachfit.sync.application.service.GarminWebhookService
comment:
Handles all Garmin Health API push callbacks.

Responsibilities (this layer):

  1. Look up CoachFit user by Garmin {@code userAccessToken}
  2. Create a {@code sync_log} entry (status = pending)
  3. Serialize the data element to JSON and enqueue a typed job on the Redis Stream
  4. Return immediately — heavy processing is done by {@link GarminHealthProcessingService} and {@link GarminActivitySyncService} asynchronously

Per docs/06-sync-engine-spec.md §Push Processing: if the user is not found by {@code userAccessToken}, return 200 + log warning, do not retry.

Per docs/06-sync-engine-spec.md §Error Handling: parse errors log but do not block acknowledgment.

methods.name: handleDailies
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_dailies} job. Worker will upsert {@code health_daily_summaries (source='garmin')} and auto-update {@code wellness_logs}: resting_hr, stress_level.

methods.name: handleActivities
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_activity} job. Worker will normalize → dedup → calculate metrics → store in {@code activities}.

methods.name: handleActivityDetails
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_activity_details} job. Worker will match the parent activity by {@code summaryId} and store {@code activity_streams}.

methods.name: handleSleep
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_sleep} job. Worker will upsert {@code health_sleep_data} and auto-update {@code wellness_logs}: sleep_hours, sleep_quality, hrv.

methods.name: handleBodyComposition
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_body} job. Worker will upsert body composition fields into {@code health_daily_summaries} and wellness weight.

methods.name: handleStress
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_stress} job. Worker will aggregate stress samples and upsert {@code health_daily_summaries.avg_stress}.

methods.name: handleHrv
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_hrv} job. Worker will upsert nightly HRV into {@code health_sleep_data.avg_hrv} and auto-update {@code wellness_logs.hrv}.

methods.name: handlePulseOx
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_pulseox} job. Worker will upsert average SpO2 into {@code health_daily_summaries.avg_spo2}.

methods.name: handleRespiration
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_respiration} job. Worker will upsert average breathing rate into {@code health_daily_summaries.avg_respiration}.

methods.name: handleUserMetrics
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_user_metrics} job. Worker will upsert VO2max into {@code health_daily_summaries.vo2max} for the most recent date.

methods.name: handleEpochs
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_epochs} job. Worker will upsert intraday epoch rows into {@code health_epoch_summaries}.

methods.name: handleBloodPressure
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_blood_pressure} job. Worker will store systolic/diastolic/pulse in {@code health_daily_summaries.extra}.

methods.name: handleMenstrualCycle
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_menstrual_cycle} job. Worker will store cycle phase and day in {@code health_daily_summaries.extra}.

methods.name: handlePregnancy
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Enqueues {@code garmin_pregnancy} job. Worker will store pregnancy data in {@code health_daily_summaries.extra}.

methods.name: handleDeregistration
methods.comment:
{@inheritDoc}

Marks each token's connection as {@code 'disconnected'} in {@code oauth_connections}. Data already synced is preserved per docs/11-privacy-compliance.md §Data Retention.

methods.name: enqueueEach
methods.comment:
Iterates over items in a push payload, looks up the user for each, and enqueues a typed job if the user is found.

Items with an unknown {@code userAccessToken} are silently dropped per docs/06-sync-engine-spec.md §Garmin push validation.

methods.name: extractToken
methods.comment: Extracts {@code userAccessToken} from a Garmin data element.

methods.name: extractSourceId
methods.comment: Extracts a best-effort source identifier for the sync log. Falls back to null if none of the known ID fields are present.

methods.name: toJson
methods.comment: Serialises a map to JSON; returns "{}" on error.

methods.name: maskToken
methods.comment: Masks a user access token for safe logging.
